verses.text: God makes this month the first of the year for Israel. New beginning. Redemption resets their very calendar.
God makes this month the first of the year for Israel. New beginning. Redemption resets their very calendar.
Each house takes a lamb, 'without blemish, a male of the first year.' The lamb. A spotless victim is chosen for every household.
Each house takes a lamb, 'without blemish, a male of the first year.' The lamb. A spotless victim is chosen for every household.
Each house takes a lamb, 'without blemish, a male of the first year.' The lamb. A spotless victim is chosen for every household.
They keep it till the fourteenth, then kill it and strike the blood on the doorposts. Blood applied. The lamb's death shelters the home.
They keep it till the fourteenth, then kill it and strike the blood on the doorposts. Blood applied. The lamb's death shelters the home.
They eat it roast with bitter herbs, ready to depart; 'it is the LORD's passover.' Eaten in haste. Redeemed people feed on the lamb, prepared to go.
They eat it roast with bitter herbs, ready to depart; 'it is the LORD's passover.' Eaten in haste. Redeemed people feed on the lamb, prepared to go.
They eat it roast with bitter herbs, ready to depart; 'it is the LORD's passover.' Eaten in haste. Redeemed people feed on the lamb, prepared to go.
They eat it roast with bitter herbs, ready to depart; 'it is the LORD's passover.' Eaten in haste. Redeemed people feed on the lamb, prepared to go.
God will smite Egypt's firstborn, but 'when I see the blood, I will pass over you.' The token. Safety is in the sign of the lamb's death.
God will smite Egypt's firstborn, but 'when I see the blood, I will pass over you.' The token. Safety is in the sign of the lamb's death.
They shall keep this feast for ever, eating unleavened bread seven days. A memorial. The deliverance is to be remembered always.
They shall keep this feast for ever, eating unleavened bread seven days. A memorial. The deliverance is to be remembered always.
They shall keep this feast for ever, eating unleavened bread seven days. A memorial. The deliverance is to be remembered always.
No leaven in their houses; whoever eats leaven is cut off. Unleavened. Redemption calls for the purging of corruption.
No leaven in their houses; whoever eats leaven is cut off. Unleavened. Redemption calls for the purging of corruption.
No leaven in their houses; whoever eats leaven is cut off. Unleavened. Redemption calls for the purging of corruption.
No leaven in their houses; whoever eats leaven is cut off. Unleavened. Redemption calls for the purging of corruption.
Moses bids the elders strike the blood with hyssop; the destroyer will not enter blood-marked doors. Sheltered. The Lord guards His blood-bought people.
Moses bids the elders strike the blood with hyssop; the destroyer will not enter blood-marked doors. Sheltered. The Lord guards His blood-bought people.
Moses bids the elders strike the blood with hyssop; the destroyer will not enter blood-marked doors. Sheltered. The Lord guards His blood-bought people.
They shall teach their children the meaning of the service; the people bow and obey. Taught and kept. Faith hands the story to the next generation.
They shall teach their children the meaning of the service; the people bow and obey. Taught and kept. Faith hands the story to the next generation.
They shall teach their children the meaning of the service; the people bow and obey. Taught and kept. Faith hands the story to the next generation.
They shall teach their children the meaning of the service; the people bow and obey. Taught and kept. Faith hands the story to the next generation.
They shall teach their children the meaning of the service; the people bow and obey. Taught and kept. Faith hands the story to the next generation.
At midnight God smites all the firstborn; 'there was a great cry in Egypt.' The blow falls. No house of Egypt is left untouched.
At midnight God smites all the firstborn; 'there was a great cry in Egypt.' The blow falls. No house of Egypt is left untouched.
Pharaoh rises and thrusts Israel out, begging them to go and even bless him. Driven out. The proud king finally yields.
Pharaoh rises and thrusts Israel out, begging them to go and even bless him. Driven out. The proud king finally yields.
Pharaoh rises and thrusts Israel out, begging them to go and even bless him. Driven out. The proud king finally yields.
They take their dough unleavened and spoil the Egyptians of jewels and raiment. Spoiled Egypt. God sends them out enriched.
They take their dough unleavened and spoil the Egyptians of jewels and raiment. Spoiled Egypt. God sends them out enriched.
They take their dough unleavened and spoil the Egyptians of jewels and raiment. Spoiled Egypt. God sends them out enriched.
About six hundred thousand men set out with a mixed multitude and flocks. The exodus. A vast host leaves at last.
About six hundred thousand men set out with a mixed multitude and flocks. The exodus. A vast host leaves at last.
About six hundred thousand men set out with a mixed multitude and flocks. The exodus. A vast host leaves at last.
After four hundred thirty years, 'even the selfsame day,' all the hosts went out. On time. God keeps His promise to the very day.
After four hundred thirty years, 'even the selfsame day,' all the hosts went out. On time. God keeps His promise to the very day.
After four hundred thirty years, 'even the selfsame day,' all the hosts went out. On time. God keeps His promise to the very day.
The passover law: no stranger or hired servant shall eat it. Set apart. The feast belongs to the covenant people.
The passover law: no stranger or hired servant shall eat it. Set apart. The feast belongs to the covenant people.
The passover law: no stranger or hired servant shall eat it. Set apart. The feast belongs to the covenant people.
It is eaten in one house, 'neither shall ye break a bone thereof.' Unbroken. The lamb foreshadows Christ, whose bones were not broken.
It is eaten in one house, 'neither shall ye break a bone thereof.' Unbroken. The lamb foreshadows Christ, whose bones were not broken.
The circumcised stranger may keep it; one law for all; God brought them out by their armies. One law. All who join the covenant share its grace.
The circumcised stranger may keep it; one law for all; God brought them out by their armies. One law. All who join the covenant share its grace.
The circumcised stranger may keep it; one law for all; God brought them out by their armies. One law. All who join the covenant share its grace.
